title: MEDIUM: Username from URL path not HTML-escaped in templates
description: In src/activitypub.rs, the username variable (extracted from Path<String>) is interpolated directly into HTML format strings without HTML escaping. Examples: line 274 '/@{username}', line 384 '@{username}@{domain}', line 361 '/@{username}/{id}', line 487 '/@{username}'. Although the username is used as a DB lookup key (so a non-existent username returns 404), and usernames are admin-controlled on this single-operator server, this is still a defense-in-depth gap. If a username were ever created containing characters like <, >, or ", it would result in HTML injection. The display_name is correctly sanitized via ammonia::clean(), but the username itself is not escaped. Recommend: apply html_attr_escape() or equivalent to username before HTML interpolation. Files: /home/mark/PROJECT/smallhold/src/activitypub.rs lines 273-274, 360-364, 384-389, 471-487.
close_reason: Fixed: username from URL path is now HTML-escaped before interpolation
